name: TMA budget CRUD surfaces
description: Add Telegram Mini App household budget management surfaces for listing budgets by household, creating monthly budgets, viewing detail/status, editing total/category limits, and deleting obsolete budgets using the existing worker budget API.

planned: Use existing worker `/api/v1/budgets` endpoints instead of adding TMA-specific API routes.
Keep TMA budget CRUD truthful to the current household-scoped worker contract: list requires `household_id`, create requires `householdId`, and group event budgets remain owned by the group feature.
Add TMA `/budgets`, `/budgets/new`, and `/budgets/:id` routes with lazy router wiring and Home shortcut access.

List household budgets, create monthly household budgets, view planned-vs-actual status from `/budgets/:id/status`, edit total/category limits with `PATCH /budgets/:id`, and delete with `DELETE /budgets/:id`.
Use reference category data and only expense categories for per-category budget limits.
verification: `pnpm --filter tma exec vitest run src/test/budget-presentation.test.ts` failed before implementation because `@/features/budgets/presentation` did not exist.
`pnpm --filter tma exec vitest run src/test/budget-detail-page.test.tsx` failed before implementation because `@/features/budgets/pages/budget-detail-page` did not exist.
`pnpm --filter tma exec vitest run src/test/budget-presentation.test.ts src/test/budget-detail-page.test.tsx` passed with 4 tests.
`jq empty harness/feature_index.json harness/features/feat-098.json` returned no output.

Final `gitnexus_detect_changes(scope: 'all', repo: 'household-finance-system')` reported `medium` risk with 47 changed symbols, 5 affected processes, and 10 indexed changed files. The affected processes reported are pre-existing TMA period picker and expense filter work already present in the dirty worktree; new budget files are not yet indexed as affected processes.

notes: Current worker budget CRUD is household-scoped. Personal budgets and first-class group budgets from the broad shared spec are not exposed by `/budgets`; group event budgets remain available through group `eventBudget`. Real Telegram WebView visual smoke remains pending because authenticated TMA launch context is required.
